summary.intent: Implement GitHub issue #289: the framework-agnostic DatePicker state machine (popover open/close, date selection, input parse/validation, value↔calendar sync, ARIA/data attrs) as a new crate module with full TDD coverage and spec sync.
summary.outcome: DatePicker agnostic core fully implemented at crates/ars-components/src/date_time/date_picker/ with 109 passing tests (unit, snapshot, spec-conformance, proptest), spec synced across 9 drift points, CLDR-backed locale date ordering wired via an exposed ars-i18n function, a pre-existing snapshot-count gate bug fixed, and a 4-commit PR opened closing #289.

repo: The Machine trait requires `type Effect: EffectName` as an associated type — string effect names and `use_platform_effects()` closures in core do not compile; focus must be adapter-driven via `PendingEffect::named(Effect::X)`, matching the Popover/Dialog convention.

CalendarDate has no PartialOrd/Ord; ordering requires `.compare(other) -> Ordering`. Field access is pub(crate); public accessors are `year() -> i32`, `month() -> u8`, `day() -> u8` (plain ints, no NonZero). `new_gregorian(i32, u8, u8) -> Result<...>`. The `nzu8` helper does not exist anywhere in the codebase.
Context for date-time components should derive only `Clone` + manual `Debug` (skip messages field) — not PartialEq — because MessageFn is not PartialEq. Calendar is the authoritative precedent.
Service::new takes its Env and Messages by reference (not by value). `send()` is #[must_use]; test code must use `drop(svc.send(...))` or bind the result.
ComponentIds has no public `base_id` field; the accessor is `ids.id()`.

`is_date_unavailable` in calendar::Props is `Option<IsDateUnavailableFn>` (a `Callback<dyn Fn(&CalendarDate)->bool + Send+Sync>`), not a bare fn pointer — must use this type when forwarding from DatePicker to calendar_props() to keep Props Clone+PartialEq.

The snapshot-count gate (`xtask/src/lint.rs` `is_component_category`) matched only hyphenated category names ('date-time') while source dirs and snapshot path segments use underscores ('date_time'), silently skipping the entire date-time and data-display categories.

ars-i18n already contained a complete private `date_order(locale) -> DateOrder` (CLDR via ICU4X, JS Intl on wasm, (language, region) fallback) at date.rs ~line 1245. DateField and DatePicker had each reimplemented only the fallback heuristic; exposing the function removed both copies.

The canonical SyncProps pattern uses `Bindable::sync_controlled(new_controlled_value)` + a `sync_props_into_ctx` helper to re-derive controlled/uncontrolled Bindables from new props while preserving uncontrolled internal state.

State (enum Open/Closed) should be the single source of truth for open state; a parallel `open: Bindable<bool>` on Context creates a divergence hazard when controlled props flip to uncontrolled — a proptest found this bug before release.

Under `default-features = false`, ars-components does not enable `ars-i18n/icu4x`; `date_order` resolves to the fallback heuristic. Under `--features i18n` it uses CLDR. Both paths agree for canonical locales, so snapshot tests are deterministic across both.
Presets (Today/Yesterday) are explicitly excluded by spec §6.2/§6.4 ('Gaps: None worth adopting'); issue test descriptions mentioning presets were author speculation, not scope.

code.finding: is_component_category matched hyphenated category strings ('date-time', 'data-display') while filesystem paths use underscores — two whole categories silently excluded from snapshot-count enforcement. Fixed by also matching underscore variants.

Private `date_order(locale) -> DateOrder` function already implements CLDR (icu_date_order), JS Intl (js_date_order), and (language, region) fallback paths. Made public to eliminate duplicate heuristic implementations in DateField and DatePicker.

segment_order_for_locale was a hand-rolled (language, region) match with a DMY default — now replaced with a call to ars_i18n::date_order. The old version agreed with CLDR for every tested locale, so no snapshot churn occurred.
CalendarDate fields are pub(crate), not public. Public API: year()->i32, month()->u8, day()->u8, compare(&Self)->Ordering, new_gregorian(i32,u8,u8)->Result. No PartialOrd/Ord, no NonZero types.
rating_group had 8 snapshots vs. a computed floor of 9 (3 parts × 3 variants). The unhighlighted item branch (item_attrs for index beyond default value) was not covered. Adding it brought it to 9/9.

workflow: Explore agents can guess APIs plausibly but incorrectly (e.g., inventing `InputChange`/`input_text`/`Control` event names). Always verify against the actual spec and source before trusting agent-synthesized API descriptions.

Property-based testing (proptest) is essential for state machines — it found a genuine Bindable divergence bug (controlled→uncontrolled flip revealing stale internal state) that unit tests missed. Run with a meaningful case count before calling the implementation done.

The post-implementation-audit skill found three categories of non-obvious issues: spec drift in derives/type aliases, a cross-category gate bug, and coverage gaps in guard branches and ConnectApi dispatch arms. Running it surfaced real problems worth fixing.

For components with a `SyncProps` event, always call `ctx.field.set(derived_value)` to keep the Bindable's internal state in sync — otherwise a controlled→uncontrolled prop flip reveals stale internal values.
Multi-commit history (logical units: drive-by fix → i18n expose → tooling → main feature) makes PRs significantly more reviewable. Stage files explicitly per commit rather than using `git add .`.

`cargo xci-fast` is the mandatory pre-push gate; it covers fmt, check, clippy, unit, integration, adapter, spec-compile-snippets, error-variant, mutual-exclusion, and snapshot-count. Pre-existing staged changes from other sessions can cause unexpected gate failures.

When a spec says 'Spec impact: No spec change required', treat it as author expectation, not a constraint — if the spec won't compile against reality, the PR must fix both code and spec in the same change per CLAUDE.md.

friction: Spec §1.7 had ~10 compile-blockers vs. the actual codebase (missing `type Effect`, string effects, CalendarDate field access, `nzu8` invention, no `Ord`, `ids.base_id`, fn vs Callback, by-value Service::new, missing on_props_changed/initial_effects) — required extensive pre-implementation grep/read verification before any code could be written.

A proptest found a real architectural bug (open Bindable diverging from State) mid-implementation, requiring a design pivot: removing the Bindable entirely and making State the single source of truth. This required touching transition arms, SyncProps, and several tests.
A stale staged change in pagination/mod.rs (from a prior session) broke xci-fast with a nightly-fmt failure that had nothing to do with the feature being implemented, blocking the pre-push gate.

The snapshot-count gate silently skipped two entire component categories for an indeterminate period due to the hyphen/underscore mismatch — a systematic gap in CI coverage that was only discovered when the fix exposed a pre-existing rating_group shortfall.
Shell backtick expansion broke a grep command mid-session, requiring a switch to the Grep tool and loss of one interactive step.
Session limit was hit mid-conversation, requiring a resume handoff.
open_items: DatePicker adapter tasks (Leptos, Dioxus) are explicitly out of scope for #289 and remain as separate issues.

Pre-existing `utility/highlight` snapshot orphans block a bare `cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject` run (feature-gated tests not run in standard CI invocations) — unrelated to this PR but noted as a pre-existing CI quirk.
94 pre-existing `cargo doc` crate-wide warnings unrelated to this change remain unfixed.
The web-intl (`js_date_order`) wasm path for the newly-exposed `date_order` function is only fully validated by the nightly wasm-coverage CI group, not by `xci-fast`.

The `Event::SyncProps(_) => None` exhaustiveness arm (mod.rs:743) is genuinely unreachable by construction (SyncProps handled before the guard match) and deliberately left at 99.84% line coverage rather than using `unreachable!()` which would panic in prod.

A proper `IntlBackend::date_order(&self, locale: &Locale) -> DateOrder` trait method (surfacing locale date order through the abstraction rather than calling the free function directly) was considered but deferred — the free function approach works for now since both components call it the same way.
